ms_camera_model.image_data package

Submodules

ms_camera_model.image_data.image_data module

Multispectral Camera Model - Image Data

  • Description: Dataclasses and their methods for image data

  • Author: Tomas Vacek

class ms_camera_model.image_data.image_data.HyperspectralImageData(img_data: ndarray, band_centers: list[float] | None = None, nbands: int | None = None)[source]

Bases: ImageData

Hyperspectral Image Data - imported from hyperspectral data file

Method import_hs_img:

Import hyperspectral cube into img_data, band_centers and nbands

classmethod import_hs_img(filepath: str) → HyperspectralImageData[source]

Import hyperspectral cube as ImageData class instance

Parameters:

filepath – path to the hyperspectral image file

Raises:

NoImageData – when spectral fails to load the image

class ms_camera_model.image_data.image_data.ImageData(img_data: ndarray, band_centers: list[float] | None = None, nbands: int | None = None)[source]

Bases: object

Image Data class

Parameters:

  • img_data – np.ndarray of image data with shape (rows, cols, bands)

  • band_centers – list of floats storing the center of each band in the img_data array

  • nbands – number of bands in the img_data array

Method __add__:

add two ImageData classes

Method imshow:

view the img_data as an RGB interpretation of selected bands or as a brightness plot

Method vector_normalize:

use vector normalization on the img_data

Method mean_spectrum_area:

calculate mean spectrum for a select area

Method plot_area_spectrum:

visualize the mean spectrum of an area

Method register_images:

register img_data of another ImageData class instance against this instance

band_centers: list[float] | None = None
img_data: ndarray
imshow(bands: list[int] | None = None) → None[source]

View image as an RGB interpretation of selected bands or as a brightness plot

Parameters:

bands – list of band numbers, if the list is not provided, brightness plot will be used instead

Raises:

IncompatibleBandChoice – if ‘bands’ is not [], len(bands) != 3 or the number is out-of-bounds

static mean_spectrum_area(img: ndarray, corner_coords: list[int]) → ndarray[source]

Calculate mean over spectral bands for select area

Parameters:

  • img – image data

  • corner_coords – coordinates of corners of the area in format [ulx, uly, lrx, lry]

nbands: int | None = None
normalize_img_data() → None[source]

Normalize image data

plot_area_spectrum(coordinates: list[int]) → None[source]

Plot spectrum of pixels

Parameters:

coordinates – list[ulx, uly, lrx, lry] - ulx means upper left x, lry means lower right y, etc.

register_bands(other: ImageData | int, band_mask_paths_ref: list[str | None] | None = None, band_mask_paths_src: list[str | None] | None = None) → ImageData | None[source]

Register img_data of another ImageData class instance against this instance

Parameters:

  • other – ImageData class instance that is being registered against this one

  • band_mask_paths_ref – list of mask file paths for reference images

  • band_mask_paths_src – list of mask file paths for source images

Raises:

ImageDataIncompatible – if the number of bands differs in self and other

vector_normalize() → None[source]

Normalize img data

class ms_camera_model.image_data.image_data.MultispectralImageData(img_data: ndarray, band_centers: list[float] | None = None, nbands: int | None = None)[source]

Bases: ImageData

Multispectral Image Data - loaded from real MS camera

Method import_altum_pt_ms_imgs:

method for import of images from MicaSense Altum-PT camera

Method import_ms_imgs:

method for import of generic multispectral images

Method check_filepaths:

method which checks that filepaths are a non-empty list and are strings

static check_filepaths(filepaths: list[str]) → None[source]

Check that provided list of filepaths is usable

Parameters:

filepaths – list of filepaths

Raises:

  • TypeError – when provided paths aren’t in list

  • TypeError – when provided paths aren’t strings

  • NoProvidedFilepaths – when there are no provided filepaths

classmethod import_altum_pt_ms_imgs(filepaths: list[str], panel_calibration: dict[str, float], panel_location: list[list[int]]) → MultispectralImageData[source]

Import and pre-process Altum PT images

Parameters:

  • filepaths – list of filepaths to the multispectral images, their order determines order in the final array

  • panel_calibration – panel_calibration data of used MicaSense CRP

  • panel_location – panel_location information format [[ulx, uly, lrx, lry], [ulx, …]]

Raises:

  • NoProvidedArea – when the provided area of the CRP is None or empty

  • Exception – when the length of filepaths and panel_locations doesn’t match

  • TypeError – when provided paths aren’t in list

  • TypeError – when provided paths aren’t strings

  • NoProvidedFilepaths – when there are no provided filepaths

classmethod import_ms_imgs(filepaths: list[str]) → MultispectralImageData[source]

Import multispectral images as a np.ndarray

Parameters:

filepaths – list of filepaths to the multispectral images, their order determines order in the final array

Raises:

  • TypeError – when provided paths aren’t in list

  • TypeError – when provided paths aren’t strings

  • NoProvidedFilepaths – when there are no provided filepaths

Module contents

Multispectral Camera Model - Image Data

Dataclasses and their methods for image data

class ms_camera_model.image_data.HyperspectralImageData(img_data: ndarray, band_centers: list[float] | None = None, nbands: int | None = None)[source]

Hyperspectral Image Data - imported from hyperspectral data file

Method import_hs_img:

Import hyperspectral cube into img_data, band_centers and nbands

classmethod import_hs_img(filepath: str) → HyperspectralImageData[source]

Import hyperspectral cube as ImageData class instance

Parameters:

filepath – path to the hyperspectral image file

Raises:

NoImageData – when spectral fails to load the image

class ms_camera_model.image_data.ImageData(img_data: ndarray, band_centers: list[float] | None = None, nbands: int | None = None)[source]

Bases: object

Image Data class

Parameters:

  • img_data – np.ndarray of image data with shape (rows, cols, bands)

  • band_centers – list of floats storing the center of each band in the img_data array

  • nbands – number of bands in the img_data array

Method __add__:

add two ImageData classes

Method imshow:

view the img_data as an RGB interpretation of selected bands or as a brightness plot

Method vector_normalize:

use vector normalization on the img_data

Method mean_spectrum_area:

calculate mean spectrum for a select area

Method plot_area_spectrum:

visualize the mean spectrum of an area

Method register_images:

register img_data of another ImageData class instance against this instance

band_centers: list[float] | None = None
img_data: ndarray
imshow(bands: list[int] | None = None) → None[source]

View image as an RGB interpretation of selected bands or as a brightness plot

Parameters:

bands – list of band numbers, if the list is not provided, brightness plot will be used instead

Raises:

IncompatibleBandChoice – if ‘bands’ is not [], len(bands) != 3 or the number is out-of-bounds

static mean_spectrum_area(img: ndarray, corner_coords: list[int]) → ndarray[source]

Calculate mean over spectral bands for select area

Parameters:

  • img – image data

  • corner_coords – coordinates of corners of the area in format [ulx, uly, lrx, lry]

nbands: int | None = None
normalize_img_data() → None[source]

Normalize image data

plot_area_spectrum(coordinates: list[int]) → None[source]

Plot spectrum of pixels

Parameters:

coordinates – list[ulx, uly, lrx, lry] - ulx means upper left x, lry means lower right y, etc.

register_bands(other: ImageData | int, band_mask_paths_ref: list[str | None] | None = None, band_mask_paths_src: list[str | None] | None = None) → ImageData | None[source]

Register img_data of another ImageData class instance against this instance

Parameters:

  • other – ImageData class instance that is being registered against this one

  • band_mask_paths_ref – list of mask file paths for reference images

  • band_mask_paths_src – list of mask file paths for source images

Raises:

ImageDataIncompatible – if the number of bands differs in self and other

vector_normalize() → None[source]

Normalize img data

class ms_camera_model.image_data.MultispectralImageData(img_data: ndarray, band_centers: list[float] | None = None, nbands: int | None = None)[source]

Bases: ImageData

Multispectral Image Data - loaded from real MS camera

Method import_altum_pt_ms_imgs:

method for import of images from MicaSense Altum-PT camera

Method import_ms_imgs:

method for import of generic multispectral images

Method check_filepaths:

method which checks that filepaths are a non-empty list and are strings

static check_filepaths(filepaths: list[str]) → None[source]

Check that provided list of filepaths is usable

Parameters:

filepaths – list of filepaths

Raises:

  • TypeError – when provided paths aren’t in list

  • TypeError – when provided paths aren’t strings

  • NoProvidedFilepaths – when there are no provided filepaths

classmethod import_altum_pt_ms_imgs(filepaths: list[str], panel_calibration: dict[str, float], panel_location: list[list[int]]) → MultispectralImageData[source]

Import and pre-process Altum PT images

Parameters:

  • filepaths – list of filepaths to the multispectral images, their order determines order in the final array

  • panel_calibration – panel_calibration data of used MicaSense CRP

  • panel_location – panel_location information format [[ulx, uly, lrx, lry], [ulx, …]]

Raises:

  • NoProvidedArea – when the provided area of the CRP is None or empty

  • Exception – when the length of filepaths and panel_locations doesn’t match

  • TypeError – when provided paths aren’t in list

  • TypeError – when provided paths aren’t strings

  • NoProvidedFilepaths – when there are no provided filepaths

classmethod import_ms_imgs(filepaths: list[str]) → MultispectralImageData[source]

Import multispectral images as a np.ndarray

Parameters:

filepaths – list of filepaths to the multispectral images, their order determines order in the final array

Raises:

  • TypeError – when provided paths aren’t in list

  • TypeError – when provided paths aren’t strings

  • NoProvidedFilepaths – when there are no provided filepaths
